reviews.review: As much as it pains me to say this - just get it on console. It's a great game, but the queue times are ridiculously long (longer than it takes to play a complete match) and it essentially requires a controller.
Great Windjammers clone, have had a lot of fun with it so far. Very tight, competitive game.
Fun game you can spend countless hours in. A game anyone can pick up and play but takes skill/time to master. Definitely recommend!

Very addictive potential to be a very competitive league game. Just need more content and more players for it to grow. Great potential.
Local + Online Multiplayer is EPIC!
It is a creative concept but quite hard to master, i recomend this game to people who have a lot of time and are able to learn from their mistakes
fantastic arcade sports game
NO
It's Windjammers.

it was working just fine. then the other day i went to hit X on my controller and invite my friend. well this is where it get s bad my keyboard wont respond at all except pressing esc and my mouse pointer goes invisable. this happens even if i use the keyboard and hit shift+tab. nothing responds. i alos hit control alt delete to bring up task manager and i have to do it 3 to 4 times till it overlays over the game of disc jam

i really really like this game but with this going on its a pain in the ass. 

ps ive uninstalled it reinstalled it 3 times now and nothign has fixed it please help

[h1]The Game Has Changed, Son Of Flynn[/h1]
Disc Jam is a multiplayer arcade sports title that pits 2-4 players against each other in a unique combination of air hockey, tennis and Disks of Tron. Players scramble to retrieve and throw a glowing disc while unleashing devastating abilities and defending variable-point end zones

[h1]Maximize efficiency, rid the new world of its imperfection![/h1]
"Disc Jam" has the right formula to become the next big breakout indie sports game. Although the matchmaking will need to improve and new content delivered regularly to prevent it from becoming just a passing fad. The devs at High Horse are addressing to expand the game. But with a high fun factor and strategic nature, despite simplicity, "Disc Jam" delivers.
[h1]Playing all the angles. End Of Line.[/h1]

What you really need in this game is teamwork and coordination because if you don't you are fucked. Second thing I got this game for free FULL version, but all they gave me was 7days of playing and now i don't even have Disc Jam
Extreme fun. Easy to get in, difficult to master. Enormously thrilling matches.

Love the game itself, but too much of the gameplay needs tweaking. LIke being hit in the back with the disc makesx since in real life and i can see it in doubles, but it is just an insta-lost in singles and me and the 6 of my friends all feel it shouldn;t be int he game at all. Along with that hitboxes don't seem to be consistant with discs just flying through characters and shields and people standing in the marking areas for lobs and net stuffs and still not catching it.

A little content is missing but great fun game!

İf you have friend to play go for it and buy this game!
It's a good game, but not worth 14.99.  There just isn't enough to it to be worth the amount listed.

[h1]"Free Weekend" review:[/h1]

Steam notified me about "Free Weekend" 30 minutes before it expires.
Completed the tutorial.
Couldn't play Online, because "license expired".
Couldn't even start couch co-op - only X input gamepads are supported. Like X-box AND... X-box... (1 out of entire pile of gamepads).

Good. I completed the tutorial. Time to unistall.

Edit: 
Cons:
- It's very arcadish and gamepads supported are limited to Xinput-only.
- You are forced to agree to a questionable "3rd party agreement" before playing - nowhere on the store page it has indication that this game has any.
- No bots. Just online matcmaking.

Pros:
+ Crossplay.
[spoiler]Yes. Crossplay.[/spoiler]

Not interested. Too arcadish for my taste. 
And a crapton of skins and taunts doesnt make the game better.

7.5/10 this game is 15 dollars it couldve been 10 but if you get it on sale for 7 its a must buy! 

Pros: fun gameplay, cross platform,really great with friends, good amount of customization but could be better

cons: repetetive with no new maps for a while, needs more features it gets a bit boring and after a certain amount of time you wont be able to play it for a long period of time only in short burts

In conclusion this game is worth it for 8     but I think 15 is a little much But anyways its good all and all
Boring
This game is pretty good in my eyes. It's nice fast and reflexive game. Would reccomend to everybody who likes this kind of genre. Also would be happy to see this game in e-sports. Good job devs.

This game is more emotional than undertale.

Desync in 2v2s with a friend and you think they miss something easy?  You'll be scared and panicked.

Play with somebody on cross-platform thats lagging to shit and you can't do anything because they're catching from across the map?  You'll be mad.

But most importantly when you win and open your prize capsule and see you got the "This is my jam" tag, you'll be happy.
Bullying baddies feels bad and getting bullied by sweaty tryhards feels bad but pegging someone in the back and watching them helplessy fall over just makes it a fun once-in-a-while distraction

It's like pong meets frisbies meets Rocket League.  Well worth it.

7 thumbs up.

I have no intention on writing anything about how the game plays or functions. What you need to know is how the developers run thier studio. This is a group of people who have distributed thier game with a charity drive event only to use it for free advertising and then took the game back from the supporters.

This game was distributed with the Yogscast Jingle Jam Humble bundle, a Charity drive event where 50K+ people donated to support multiple diffirent charities in the month of december(30$ for 50+ games was the bundle this year). The developers distributed thier game with this bundle only to remove the game from everyones library a few weeks later when the game was released claiming that the keys for the people that bought the bundle were "Alpha Only". They removed the game from the supporters library to get them to re-purchase it on release. Which caused the community to ignore them and refuse to play thier game. They had problems on release of "not enough people playing" for that reason, they took the game from 50K+ people and ruined thier opening playerbase. They had to create cross platform support because the only people who had the game were on playstation because they got it for free from playstation plus. Then they waited several monts(7) and had a "free weekend" deal when they figured everyone who had seen thier behavior had either forgotten about how they abused a charity drive for free advertisement then tried to make people repurchase the game, or stopped paying attention to what they were doing.

Do Not Support this kind of behavior in any human ever.

Edit: people asked for a screenshot for proof
http://imgur.com/DlrM8YS
http://imgur.com/3qX2JYQ

Ok where do i start off from this? Honestly this is a very interesting game, its tennis but with a disc you have your net, arena, and players however i've been very skeptical for past few days of multiplayer games for the past year because lately they'll be shoved off the buying line cause the devs feel as though it should be free which is good but also bad if the product hasn't been finished at all yet and they'll send causual updates to fix the game. But this game is very interesting unique and fun, you have guard points for disc ball, power abilities, and other things the sliding is tricky to master but you can adjust to your key perfomance for key bindings other than as long as the devs keep this updated, finished, and fixed no issues here as long as its not shoved off the buying market i'll keep an eye on this game as well as my wishlish. Good day devs.

I'm amazed at how well the game is balanced. 9/10.
Would've given 10/10 if there was more stuff and sometimes the lag is so bad you think the other player is hacking.
PROS:
*Fun game. Never bores you out!
*Amazing visuals.
*Optimized even for potato PC's
*Stress relieving, easy to learn and play but takes considerable time to master
*Has cross play with consoles so finding a game was never been easier.
*Has 2 player , local.

CONS:
*Music is reapeatetive.
*Once the skill level is high, you will turn salty if you're weak.
*Single game type but will be improved in the not too distant future or I hope so.
*Had blisters from trying to spin.
*A joystick is a must for PC players

9/10 Would want to get blisters again.

Great competitive game for friends and even online. It's easy to learn from the tutorials and the characters feel different. Only one stage but the stage doesn't effect the game so it doesn't really matter to me. Also it is no longer dead

If you like Rocket League give this game a try! i played 7 hours non stop it was really intense my thumb is hurting ! i can't even remember the last time this happened lol -_-

This game is so much fun. Reminds me of the old Discs Of Tron arcade game. Easy to pick up hard to master similar to rocket league. Totally worth the price.

8.5/10
I picked this game up on the free weekend after having seen a few Rocket League streamers play it on their free time and, boy, i was not dissapointed. The amount of laughs and pure shenanigans that happen in this game are unprecedented! Its generally easy to pick up and play and the skill curve (not the throw) is quite high. I can see myself putting in a decent amount of time into this game as there is always something to learn and a new way to play! The only downsides this game has are its small community which makes matchmaking a tad long but not unbearable and the fact that there is only really one way to play so its possible to get bored after a while but if you play with a freind this game will entertain for hours and hours.

The game seems great. However the one that does not have support for generic gamepad blocked many experiences. I know how to play very well with the keyboard, but I did feel that a lot of playability was almost impossible to do for someone without xbox one controller and with little keyboard skill. It looks great to play with friends and has possibilities like eSport. At the moment (as long as it do not have support for generic gamepad) I am not interested in buying it, but I will be aware of any updates. Maybe if it's worth the full price. n.n
if you like frisbee buy it

Great game, incredibly addicting, but I don't see me playing it more than the bit I get on this free weekend. There's no gameplay differential besides the singles/doubles and I don't see ranking/skill anywhere. As a meh-skilled player, playing against other meh-skilled peeps, the scoring is a bit wank. It's awesome that the point value increases as the length of the point increases (for the pro and sober types), but at the low levels, we all fuck up on the simplest shots and win or lose based on a random long rally or slowly lose due to the 10pt ace vs the 6pt return "winner".

Tried it out for the free weekend.  Played the tutorial and some random matches, pretty dope.   Tried to invite a friend to queue up for doubles, couldn't do it.  Tried to invite a friend to play a private match, couldn't do it.  9/10 for gameplay, 0/10 for group play.  In the end crappy game where you can't play with friends.
fun game.
Amazing game had to buy it after trying the free weekend! Such a great game with an amazing skill ceiling.

This game is def worth playing. Its no rocket league but its def a game to own and play. Its highly competitive and it takes real skill to master the technique. The game is just $14.99 so its hard to pass this one up

Played the free weekend. I won one game and lost one game. The first game I played the other guy didn't actually do anything besides ocassionally fidget to the left and right. The second game I played I won 1:2 having a little bit of fun though becoming frustrated by the dumbass 6 button system. The second game it went from disc throwing to fucking mario power tennis with the guy throwing the ball "perfect" which is basically setting the ball up for yourself in volleyball like a dumbass but you cant actually spike or really counter the set up so you just watch yourself get spiked. The other thing is if youre not facing the ball you have to slide because I guess whoever made this has never been outside or cant turn their head and cant fathom catching an object without directly facing it.

The actual quality of the game is minimal. It looks like trash and the servers suck but at least it runs without crashing (at least for me). The characters look fucking stupid but thats whatever.

Basically it should be a crime to charge money for this but if it went F2P I'd recommend it.

Just buy it, don't even think. It's awesome as hell, specially if you were a fan of Windjammers on the arcades! Barely had any problems with lag, as some reviews seem to point out, maybe their internet is just bad. Also with the introduction of crossplay there are more than enough players to keep the matchmaking fast and smooth!

For the price it's a ton of fun in this tiny package.  Not a whole lot in the game but it's easy to learn and tough to master.  I thought I'd only play for a half an hour and move on during the free weekend but I ended up playing for hours until I had to leave for work.

Amazing game im already hooked and im pretty sure the devs will constantly update it in the future to give the fans what they want similarly to psyonix and their rocket league.

Much love!
It's funny how the creators sent a copy to everyone registered on their newsletter and then take it away... I feel robbed lol

I picked it up because of the weekend trial and within ten minutes, I became disillusioned. I expected the game to be a balance of competitive casualness, something akin to Rocket League. What I got was a fun premise with the execution of a Cartoon Network flash game. As soon as the game loaded, I'm told the game is optimized for Xbox controllers, an early death knell. Keyboard controls are obstrusive, a fatal error for a game on Steam, a market exclusively for PC. For what it's worth, the game does a lot of things first. It's the first game I played to lose local connection in Training Mode, the first to have a long loading screen for the main menu after a match, the first game where keyboard control schemes hobble players,  the first to have a poorly implemented tutorial. For keyboarders, there are only three possible directions for the disc to be thrown (A W D). Good luck throwing that disc with any sense of accuracy or precision if you don't roll controller. Controllers have five possible directions due to sticks. This game really doesn't favor this market and would be better off exclusively on consoles.
excellent game ! worth a buy ! best thing you can rek PS4 players and trash talk them and they cant even reply! SO AWSOME !

Pros: meh, mildly entertaining

Cons:
*Angles don't work quite right all the time on the joystick.  With dashing or throwing.
*Latency is too key for perfect throws, if you get even a little bit high ping then its over for you.
*The physics of the throws don't always make sense. Sometimes the angle is clear and it touches a wall and goes ape shit.
* The point system is bogus, every shot doesn't count for the same amount of points and it should.  A Tennis style point system would have made way more sense.

This is literally one of the worst ports.

No mouse.
Uses WASD to in place of a gamepad if you have one, making it impossible to even get past the tutorial.

Tried it out during a free weekend.
Wasn't worth the time I spent trying to get around the tutorial.
Isn't worth the money.

I tried this out on a free weekend.

It was okay, but the graphics settings were set to medium which my rig barely kept up with 30fps.  I have 2 cards in SLI and the second card never went above 5% usage.  Which, okay, but in medium the textures look rubbish.  Obviously a console port.

Additionally, once i went through the tutorial I was matchmaked with someone who had obviously played the game a lot more, I got slaughtered.  So there is obviously no matchmaking, just random opponents.

The game looks okay, its basically a tennis game in the style of Tron.

The game may be fun enough but i would recommend this only for consoles as its obviously designed for that and you'll get a better product.  If the matchmaking doesn't improve I can imagine the games would be unbalanced most of the time.

Not for me, I think

The controls are too clunky (in comparisson with Virtua Tennis series, for example).
In overall, the game seems way less polished as it should be.

Disc Jam, its pretty cool but I strongly recommend a controller to play.  I dont know how the player base is during normal play time, but given the complaints of how the game is usually dead when its not a free weekend, thats something to keep in mind when thinking about getting the game. It may not be very populated under normal circumstances. Other than those two thoughts though I think Disc Jam is pretty neat.
Unplayable without a controller

garbage game. wouldn't even play it for free.

Once was needed rotating stick before throw (X), and second very fast while holding X.  Also tried 10 times both sequence and it works on 70% (which is like "I would like to throw like this...pleaaase")...

Trying this during the free weekend. This feels just like Rocket leauge..in the way its easy to drop in an play and the game is really fun and short. The coop mode can be a bit lol. If you get two pros and you have someone new on your side..It can be really tough, Maybe bigger arenas with more people and rules will make it more interesting for coop. But for 1v1,the system is perfect. Different arenas could be nice as well.   8/10

This game is Great. I saw it on a stream and the bought it right away and brought a friend with to play it and we had alot of fun. The cross play really helps out the player base and im glad to see it free and on sale this weekend.
Boomerang War. its good fun :)

I tried the game while it was/is free for the weekend. I have played about 8 hours non-stop and am loving the competitive side of it. Hopefully the developers take the ranking system seriously and professionally and I could see this game being a solid 1V1/2V2 competitive game.

The game is extremly fast paced and can be played easily by anyone, but requires fast reactions and a lot of game knowledge to master the art.

With all this being said, there are a couple of cons to be mentioned.
The first and most annoying thing to most PC players is the controlls. I use an Xbox controller so the controls work well for me but I have read a fair amount of complaints about the issue.
The game skins are a bit gimicky and its quite cartoonish.

Otherwise this game is great fun. I play on Oceanic servers in australia and seem to be fine from any lag issues.
good game and much fun

Its pretty cool, but as it stands it was too laggy connection wise for me to play. By the time I saw the other player move I was being scored on. I'm in US east with a decent connection so I don't think it is on my end. Would really like to see some improvements in netcode, as it sits its basically unplayable.

Why would you make a game for PC/Steam and have it /only/ work with controllers??
Yes, I realise that you can play the game with keyboard, but when it doesn't allow you to even complete the tutorial because "Controllers are recommended" (I had to hit targets that I physically couldn't hit with keyboard controls) why even bother doing a PC release? Just release it for consoles and leave it like that...
Like surely, if they were adamant on doing a PC release, they could have utilised the mouse to assist with fine tuning your throwing. But, nope... Still recommending controllers...

And its a shame because I've watched gameplay of youtubers playing this (Cartoonz and Delirious) and it looked really fun.
what the fuck is this pile of garbage.
excellent game, wish alot more people would play this.

Disc Jam is a fun game but not a game I would spend money on. It has a cool concept and as far as I am concerned is pretty unique for a video game. Overall the game can be frusterating however, one of my main dislikes is the resset to the middle every goal. In Disc Jam you will find that goals are scored alot so when you have to respawn to the middle everytime you score it is quite annoying overtime. I think this could easily be fixe to improve the overall flow of gameplay. Another big problem of Disc Jam is the keyboard controls, or what is supposed to be keyboard controls... Don't play this game if you don't have a controller. Period.

Overall it is a fun game that I never would of known about if it wasn't for a free weekend, thanks to the devs for letting me try this fun little game but personally I do not think it fits the price tag as it lacks alot where content should be, it only comes with - one map, four characters, one game mode. I feel this would get very repetitive after little play, and eventually plain out boring. I personally  hope in the future this game will become f2p as I think most other people would agree 15 bucks is far too much for this little content.

I played it briefly. It was pretty and OK. Keyboard controls were awful. No mouse support either. I don't have a game controller and can't use one due to my hand disabilities. :( Either way, the game is not bad. It just needs better usability.

Controller=Great                   Keyboard=Impossible

I've been following this game for a bit now and was excited to see it on sale with a free weekend. I jumped on the opportunity to play it and had high hopes for the player base to expand and make matchmaking much better. 

Sadly, I quicly found out this game is currently unplayable with a keyboard. Basic mechanics such as aiming are virtually impossible with just WASD, and this really limits the player base that this game needs to be great. If you have a controller, I reccomend this, but if you only have access to a keyboard: stay away.

I really hope the developers can see this complaint and fix the contollers with some sort of mouse mechanic.

I've played this for about 20 minutes and already it's incredibly addiciting. Was just trying out free weekend, but they just got a buyer here. It's basically some hardcore tennis and frisbee combined with some SNES Super Dodgeball thrown in. I haven't had to wait over a few seconds to join a game, but then again it is a free weekend. Haven't had a single drop of lag either, even when I play on EU servers (I'm US.)
It sucks. Make it work for keyboard and mouse. Until then, it is a pile of shit.
loved this game very fun gotta hand it to the dev team who created this they did some real good work
So much fun, reminds me of virtual tennis..

This is really close to a great Windjammers clone but the small pieces make it fall apart.

The collision detection isn't great which is frustrating.

There is a pretty cool block ability that will mack the disc in the air, except its the same button as throw so often when you think you have the disc you block to early and block breaking your rhythm.

If the disc hits you from behind it knocks you down. I get what they were going for but it really doesn't fit with the pace of the game.
the game's pretty good so far. it just needs more content so we can have an incentive to play it again and again

There might be a problem with the playerbase but i doubt it since they updated the game with the crossplay update and this free weekend may entice more people to try it out, i know it did for me and i enjoyed the experience and am going to buy the game.

Solid 8.0/10 Experience (Possibly an 8.5 if they add new characters and modes)
